articles.headline: Every team's championship, group odds for the 2026 World Cup

images.caption: Cape Verde are -230 to qualify from Group H.
images.credit: Carmen Mandato - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Spain [1296x729]
images.caption: Spain is tied for second-favorite with England, as La Roja head into their second match.
images.alt: FerrÃ¡n Torres #7 of Spain dribbles during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match between Spain and Cape Verde on June 15th, 2026 at Atlanta Stadium in Atlanta, GA.

images.name: USMNT Mauricio Pochettino and Weston McKennie March8-2026 [1296x729]

images.caption: Portugal saw their World Cup odds lengthen following Wednesday's 1-1 draw with Congo DR.

images.name: Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappe and France are favorites to win their third World Cup.

images.name: Felix Nmecha [576x324]
images.caption: Felix Nmecha and Germany open strong against Curacao.
images.alt: Felix Nmecha #23 of Germany celebrates his goal by pretending to take off his crown during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E match.

images.name: Vinicius Jr. [1296x729]
images.caption: With a 1-1 draw, Brazil saw their odds to win the World Cup lengthen from +950 to 11-1.
images.alt: Vinicius Junior of Brazil runs with the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium on June 13, 2026 in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
images.credit: Shaun Botterill - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Yamale for WC Odds file (260529) [1296x729]
images.caption: Spain's World Cup fortunes are tied to the health of Lamine Yamal's left leg.

article.headline: Salah leads rally over New Zealand as Egypt gets first-ever WC win
article.description: Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal, and Egypt went on to defeat New Zealand 3-1 on Sunday night and secure a first-ever World Cup win.
article.linkText: Salah leads rally as Egypt get first-ever WC win

video.title: Ogden: Mo Salah showed his class in Egypt's first World Cup win

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC Analysis (Ogden: Mo Salah showed his class in Egypt's first World Cup win) 2026/6/21

article.story:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia -- Mohamed Salah scored the go-ahead goal, and Egypt went on to defeat New Zealand 3-1 on Sunday night and secure a first-ever World Cup win.

Salah celebrated his 68th international goal by pumping his fist before he was mobbed by his teammates in the 67th minute, to the thrill of the red-clad Pharaohs fans in the sellout crowd at BC Place Vancouver.

When Salah was subbed off in the 85th minute, he was treated to a standing ovation.

Egypt moved to the top of the standings in Group G with the win, but were not yet assured a spot in the knockout round. The Pharaohs play a deciding game against Iran on Friday in Seattle, needing at least a draw to advance.

Egypt are making their fourth World Cup appearance after missing the field in Qatar four years ago. Salah scored two goals in the 2018 World Cup in Russia.

Finn Surman put New Zealand in front in the 15th minute with a flying header off a corner kick delivered by Instagram sensation Tim Payne. It was the third international goal for the 6-foot-3 defender, who plays for the Portland Timbers in MLS.

Omar Marmoush dropped a free kick off to Salah in the 35th minute, but it went just wide and bent into the side netting.

Egypt turned up the pressure coming out of the half, and Mostafa Zico broke through with an equalizer, finding space between Surman and Payne for a header that goalkeeper Max Crocombe got a hand on but couldn't stop in the 58th minute. Zico celebrated by gesturing for the fans in the crowd to cheer.

Salah took a back-heel pass from Zico for his goal, which puts him just one from tying his coach Hossam Hassan for the national team's all-time scoring lead. Trézéguet's diving header wrapped up scoring for Egypt in the 82nd.

Egypt's Hamdy Fathy was subbed off late in the first half after an apparent injury away from the ball. He was replaced by Ramy Rabia.

In the opener against Belgium, Emam Ashour scored the early goal for Egypt, but then Romelu Lukaku was subbed into the match and caused chaos in the box, resulting in an own goal for a 1-1 draw.

New Zealand twice took the lead against Iran in their group opener but ceded it for a 2-2 draw. Elijah Just scored both goals for the All Whites.

New Zealand previously appeared at the World Cup in 2010 and drew all three of their games. They return to Vancouver on Friday to face Belgium.

videos.headline: Salah sinks Kiwi hearts in Egypt's 3-1 win
videos.description: Mohamed Salah stars as Egypt pile on three goals in a dominant second-half performance after Finn Surman's header gave New Zealand an early lead.

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: blockedShots

stats.name: effectiveClearance
stats.description: The number of clearances that stops the attack of the opposing team.
